guidance.repeat_testing_guidance: Washington guidance should drive repeat testing and follow-up timing. No universal Sharp v1 cadence is assumed beyond current state health context and lab guidance.
guidance.benchmark_notes: Washington uses state PFAS action-level and drinking-water context for interpretation. Private well PFAS results remain reference-based and should not be framed as household compliance determinations.

profile.primary_reference_label: Washington PFAS state action levels aligned to federal MCLs
profile.summary: Washington historically used PFAS state action levels and, effective January 15, 2026, aligned those SAL values with the federal PFAS MCL values while maintaining state monitoring and trigger logic.

benchmarks.label: Washington SAL for PFHxS
benchmarks.note: Federal MCL is scheduled at 4 ppt in 2029, but current state SAL is 10 ppt.

benchmarks.label: Washington SAL for PFNA
benchmarks.note: Aligned state action level remains 10 ppt.

benchmarks.label: Washington SAL for HFPO-DA
benchmarks.note: Added as part of Washington's PFAS transition to the federal structure.

profile.private_well_use_note: Washington private-well interpretation can borrow the current SAL context, but the output should still emphasize state guidance and accredited-lab follow-up rather than imply a public-water compliance result.

entry_decision_input.summary: No direct well test is available yet, so the engine should route the household to a state-guided lab and sampling workflow before any product comparison.
